highlights.title: People Living Near xAI’s Dirty Data Centers Are Furious About the SpaceX IPO
highlights.description: Elon Musk is set to make hundreds of billions even as communities in Mississippi and Tennessee are fighting to stop the gas turbines powering xAI's supercomputers.

highlights.title: SpaceX IPO Will Mint Billions for a New Silicon Valley Hierarchy
highlights.description: The initial public offering for SpaceX is poised to generate billions of dollars in profits for the fortunate few investors who got in early on Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence company.

highlights.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, other U.S. AI giants launch major London expansions amid talent and revenue scramble
highlights.description: The U.K. capital has become a key growth target for many of the world's most talked about AI companies.

highlights.title: OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ
highlights.description: OpenAI is reportedly mulling drastic price cuts to its AI models, as it looks to woo consumers from rival AI company Anthropic, the WSJ reported on Wednesday.

highlights.title: AI mega-listings are 'just the start,' Razer CEO says, ahead of historic SpaceX IPO
highlights.description: Blockbuster public offerings from AI companies could be an enduring feature of the industry, Razer CEO Min-Liang Tan said Thursday, ahead of several mega IPOs.

highlights.title: Voyager CEO: SpaceX IPO Attention Good for Space Sector
highlights.description: The week SpaceX launches its highly-anticipated initial public offering. Chairman and CEO of Voyager Technologies Dylan Taylor says ultimately more attention on the space sector will be good for the industry as a whole. Taylor spoke to Bloomberg TV's Francine Lacqua on Wednesday June 10. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: SpaceX IPO Good for Sector, Says Voyager CEO
highlights.description: On Friday, SpaceX launches its highly-anticipated initial public offering in what is set to be the largest listing in history. Dylan Taylor, Chairman and CEO of aerospace and defense company Voyager Technologies says he's "bullish and optimistic" about the impact it will have on the industry, saying he believes there will be a lot of capital that "flows back into other space names as well" . In an interview with Francine Lacqua on Bloomberg TV on Wednesday June 10, Taylor also discussed plans for habitation on the moon, the importance of the Artemis mission as well as the space race.  (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
highlights.description: If you doubted his genius, doubt no more.

highlights.title: OpenAI says fake accounts from China tried to turn Americans against data centers
highlights.description: OpenAI has published a report detailing how China-linked influence campaigns against data centers used ChatGPT.

highlights.title: Upstart chipmakers keep challenging Nvidia. This time it's Microsoft-backed D-Matrix
highlights.description: Nvidia challenger D-Matrix is entering full production of an AI chip it says is 10 times faster than a GPU and bypasses the memory shortage.

business.title: Oracle beats on earnings, but stock drops on plans to raise another $20 billion
business.description: Oracle topped quarterly expectations, but free cash flow is negative, and the company plans to raise more capital for data center projects.

business.title: Italian Unicorn Satispay Plans €120 Million Funding Round
business.description: Satispay is planning to raise as much as €120 million ($139 million) to fund the Italian fintech’s expansion into new financial products and give it the flexibility to pursue acquisitions.

ai.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
ai.description: The company changed course after researchers spoke out against the policy, which would have covertly limited Claude’s ability to develop competing AI models.

ai.title: AI Sparks Alarm in China With Call to Protect Worker Rights
ai.description: China’s rapid adoption of artificial intelligence in the workplace has prompted an unusually blunt call from a state-run newspaper to protect labor rights, as Beijing considers how to contain risks posed by the new technology.

ai.title: Louis Rossmann is suing Samsung after firm offers $330 refund for defective SSD while selling the drives on Amazon for $949 — spat over 4TB 990 Pro SSD is headed to court
ai.description: Right to Repair activist Louis Rossman threatens to sue Samsung after the SSD maker failed to replace his dead 990 Pro 4TB SSD under warranty.

ai.title: Anthropic backtracks on policy that 'sabotaged' researchers' work
ai.description: Anthropic is walking back a hidden policy that researchers say sabotaged their work.

ai.title: US-Backed Philippines AI Hub Could Draw at Least $10 Billion in Investments
ai.description: A US-backed plan to build an artificial intelligence hub in the Philippines could draw an initial investment of about $10 billion, an official said, and help prop up an economy battered by the Iran war.

ai.title: YouTube is introducing DMs (again)
ai.description:
You have to be over 18 and already be connected with the person you want to chat with on other messaging platforms. | Illustration by Alex Castro / The Verge	

YouTube is reintroducing private messaging after testing new ways for users to share videos and "have conversations about them" last year. In an announcement on its official blog, YouTube says it's now starting to expand the in-app video sharing and messaging feature to users in the US and "other global regions" who are 18 or older.
The video sharing platform had previously introduced private messaging in 2017, before discontinuing it just two years later to focus on public conversations like the Instagram-like Stories feature - which it also killed in 2023. When it started testing its latest in-app messaging capabilities in November 2025,  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

ai.title: Warren Wants Wall Street to Disclose Data on AI Firm Exposure
ai.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren introduced a bill requiring financial institutions to disclose their exposure to artificial intelligence companies, continuing her push for more scrutiny on the sector’s funding.

ai.title: Europe Margins Poised for First Growth Since 2022 on Oil, AI
ai.description: European companies’ margins are set to expand for the first time since 2022, driven by soaring commodity prices and artificial intelligence.

ai.title: Apple’s iPadOS 27 beta downloads briefly included two unsupported iPad Pro models
ai.description: Apple briefly listed iPadOS 27 beta 1 restore images for two older iPad Pro models before removing them from its developer downloads page. Here are the details.

ai.title: Deezer launches an AI music detector for other streaming services
ai.description:
Deezer will now scan your playlists on other streaming platforms to detect AI-generated music. Deezer was the first of the big streaming services to start labeling AI-generated music. It even offered its tech to other platforms, but it doesn't seem like it had many buyers. Qobuz launched its own detection tech, while Apple and Spotify have opted for a voluntary tagging system. 
"No other company has followed our lead yet, so we decided to make it possible for everyone to check if their playlists include synthetic music, no matter which streaming platform they use," Deezer CEO Alexis Lanternier said in a press release. So, since nobody is ta …
Read the full story at The Verge.

ai.title: NS&I dangles £220K salary for CEO willing to straighten out £3B IT mess
ai.description: Comes with a corner office, government scrutiny, and the 'full-spectrum disaster' known as Project Rainbow

ai.title: Every employee’s password was stored in a single Excel file
ai.description: The CEO thought this was the best way to deal with some email issues

chips_cloud.title: Upstart chipmakers keep challenging Nvidia. This time it's Microsoft-backed D-Matrix
chips_cloud.description: Nvidia challenger D-Matrix is entering full production of an AI chip it says is 10 times faster than a GPU and bypasses the memory shortage.

chips_cloud.title: Oracle Falls as Data Center Costs Exceed Estimates
chips_cloud.description: Oracle shares declined in premarket trading after the company reported quarterly capital expenses that were higher than estimates. The company expects to spend about $70 billion on net capital expenditures in the current fiscal year, which ends in May 2027. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham has more.  (Source: Bloomberg)

chips_cloud.title: TDK US CEO on Plans to Acquire Fabric8Labs
chips_cloud.description:
Jim Tran, CEO of TDK USA, discusses the company's plans to acquire US startup Fabric8Labs, as part of the firm's broader push into its data center business.
He speaks exclusively with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade".
 (Source: Bloomberg)

chips_cloud.title: AI is set to consume up to 600 billion gallons of water by 2030 — rising energy consumption primarily to blame as data center power demands rise
chips_cloud.description: Direct cooling data center GPUs uses only a fraction of the water required to keep them running, and with plans for future GPUs and rack systems to be even more power hungry, this problem could make data centers even more of a resource hog.

ev_autonomous.title: Live updates: Bitcoin nears $63,000 ahead of highly anticipated SpaceX IPO
ev_autonomous.description: May's inflation numbers lifted major digital assets on Thursday, though ether and the large altcoins are still down 6% to 8% over seven days.

ev_autonomous.title: Welcome to the Waymo World Cup
ev_autonomous.description: It might not feel all that different from older World Cups—for better or worse.

ev_autonomous.title: Grindr CEO Wants His Dating App to Become a Gay Culture Hub
ev_autonomous.description: Madonna made a surprise appearance in Times Square last week, promoting her latest album, Confessions II. It was a marketing win for the pop star — and an even bigger one for Grindr Inc.
